inline_comments.message:
Please verify the filenames in k8s-datafile.yaml against those used everywhere else in the other files. They appear to be different.
If correct, perhaps a comment would be worth adding to k8s-datafile.yaml saying that they are flipped from what is used elsewhere for the reason ....

please verify these. they appear to be opposite from those specified in

k8s-datafile.yaml:
        dmaap.ftp.ftpesConfiguration.keyCert: "/config/ftp.jks"
        dmaap.ftp.ftpesConfiguration.trustedCA: "/config/dfc.jks"

please verify these. They appear to be opposite from what was specified in k8s-datafile.yaml:

        dmaap.ftp.ftpesConfiguration.keyCert: "/config/ftp.jks"
        dmaap.ftp.ftpesConfiguration.trustedCA: "/config/dfc.jks"

please verify these. They appear to be opposite from what was specified in k8s-datafile.yaml:

        dmaap.ftp.ftpesConfiguration.keyCert: "/config/ftp.jks"
        dmaap.ftp.ftpesConfiguration.trustedCA: "/config/dfc.jks"

please verify these. They appear to be opposite from what was specified in k8s-datafile.yaml:

        dmaap.ftp.ftpesConfiguration.keyCert: "/config/ftp.jks"
        dmaap.ftp.ftpesConfiguration.trustedCA: "/config/dfc.jks"

please verify these. They appear to be opposite from what was specified in k8s-datafile.yaml:

        dmaap.ftp.ftpesConfiguration.keyCert: "/config/ftp.jks"
        dmaap.ftp.ftpesConfiguration.trustedCA: "/config/dfc.jks"
The file names are now corrected in this file to the proper value. The other comments were related to this and does not require modification.
